commands.review_summary:
The branch adds pre-normalization quarantine for unserializable active runtime, bundled MCP, and bundled LSP tool schemas, logs diagnostics, and adds focused projection/quarantine regression tests.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +162. Total +245 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection and the branch tests give a high-signal reproduction shape: a circular tool parameter schema reaches provider normalization before current-main runtime projection. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid runtime tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

Pruned the runtime tool-schema quarantine branch down to the assistant-turn failure path.

This adds a pre-normalization quarantine for active runtime tools whose parameter schema cannot be serialized safely before provider normalization. Healthy sibling tools continue into provider normalization, and the existing runtime-compatible projection still filters model/runtime-incompatible schemas before session creation.

The test fixtures use neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed on head `72bafda8a90`: 3 test files, 64 tests, 22.73s.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- Diff scrub for private paths, install-error output, and the originally reported fixture name passed.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed on current main `1fd9fe2b33a`: run `run_c327e2a6f018`, lease `cbx_075cee1f353c` / `swift-krill`, provider `aws`, head `72bafda8a90`, command 3m10s, total 5m10s.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` exited clean before the latest rebase with no accepted/actionable findings. It noted compaction as a sibling follow-up outside this branch's intentionally pruned scope.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A single active tool with a schema that cannot survive provider/runtime schema projection is quarantined before assistant session creation instead of crashing the whole assistant turn.

Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest/format/lint proof through repo wrappers; remote Linux changed gate through AWS Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Verification above.

Evidence after fix: Focused runner coverage shows session creation receives only the healthy sibling tool when one active tool has a non-serializable schema; projection/quarantine unit coverage verifies provider-repairable schemas are preserved and non-serializable schemas are reported.

Observed result after fix: Healthy tools continue; the bad tool is reported through the existing trusted diagnostic/quarantine path.

What was not tested: Live provider calls. Manual/overflow/timeout compaction tool normalization is not changed in this PR and should stay as a separate follow-up hardening PR if we want the same quarantine boundary there.
target.author: vincentkoc
